text: O fun bu ŋɔ ba, “Ba wa kpa gar fali aa lik takta buni bɔ di o vusi. Ba ka kpa gar fali ti a lik takta buni ti, o zi bukoo chɛɛsi kibuni tiu a gbaari.
translated: "The saying goes: 'She does not eat raw meat and licks the plate with salt, yet she dies. He does not eat the raw meat and licks the plate with it, and he becomes very sick from the salt in the plate.'"

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Nara tima maga funɛ kaali, o la zu be ti dɛ chɔɔli ti a kpak o nin. O funɛ ya chene, be ti vaan si o sou ni.
translated: If you do something wrong, he/she will no longer want to stay in this house. He/She will write your name and include you in his/her book.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: amaa ya wara daŋni ya muksi nara nama. Ma la bɛɛŋ ti a la yo konkunte a kpaksi nyinsa. Nyine naa ma wa te kpak, ma zi na malɛu o nɔ ni. Malɛ ta zi iiu a bɔ mu awa hin Koosa bar jɛɛmi dam lampo. Kpa a a la bɔ lampo nilalɛ ta.”
translated: Amaa said to Wara Daŋni, "Teach the child Nama. But he did not want to listen to his word. Nyine came to eat, but he did not want to go to Malɛu. Malɛ took his hand and led him to Koosa and the village of the lamp. He said, 'He did not take his inheritance's lamp.'"

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Nihaan tima funɛ nyɛn nyinbaan ni aa la, lanjimasa ba naa funɛ il hugil ti bɔnɛ, fun a bur wa tɔhɔn tuha, a wa ŋɔ Koosa dam kɔkɔɔrsi ta kina maga yɛɛ.
translated: In Nihaan, the child of the chief was born, and the elders were there, and the people of Koosa village gathered.
